# CLAUDE.md

Guidance for working in this repository.

## Project

**MyBlogClient** — an Angular 21 blog front-end with server-side rendering (SSR). Generated by Angular CLI 21.2.12; currently early-stage scaffolding (routes are empty, feature work under `src/user/` is in progress and untracked).

## Commands

- `npm start` — dev server at `http://localhost:4200/` (alias for `ng serve`).
- `npm run build` — production build to `dist/` (SSR output mode).
- `npm run watch` — development build with `--watch`.
- `npm test` — run unit tests with **Vitest** (`@angular/build:unit-test`).
- `npm run serve:ssr:MyBlogClient` — run the built SSR server (`node dist/MyBlogClient/server/server.mjs`).
- `ng generate component <name>` — scaffold a component.

There is no configured e2e framework. Run a single spec via the Vitest runner through `ng test` (no separate filter flag is wired up yet).

## Architecture

- **SSR / hydration**: Express server in `src/server.ts` hosts the Angular app via `AngularNodeAppEngine`. Client bootstrap is `src/main.ts`; server bootstrap is `src/main.server.ts`. Hydration uses `provideClientHydration(withEventReplay())` in `src/app/app.config.ts`.
- **Server rendering**: `src/app/app.routes.server.ts` prerenders all routes (`path: '**'`, `RenderMode.Prerender`). Listens on `PORT` env var, default `4000`.
- **Routing**: client routes live in `src/app/app.routes.ts` (currently empty). Root component is `App` (`src/app/app.ts`, selector `app-root`).
- **Feature code**: organized under `src/` by area, e.g. `src/user/layout/` (the `Layout` component). Component selector prefix is `app`.

## Conventions

This project enforces strict Angular v20+/v21 conventions. (Also mirrored in `best-practices.md` and `instructions.md`, which add a persona and worked examples.)

### TypeScript

- Strict type checking (enforced via `tsconfig.json`).
- Prefer type inference when the type is obvious.
- Avoid `any`; use `unknown` when the type is uncertain.

### Components

- **Standalone components** only — never `NgModule`. Do NOT set `standalone: true` in the `@Component`/`@Directive`/`@Pipe` decorators (it's the default in v20+).
- Set `changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` on every component.
- Use the `input()` and `output()` functions, not `@Input`/`@Output` decorators.
- Keep components small and single-responsibility.
- Prefer inline templates for small components; for larger ones keep TS logic, HTML template, and CSS in separate files (`*.ts` / `*.html` / `*.css`). When using external templates/styles, use paths relative to the component TS file.
- Prefer Reactive forms over Template-driven.
- `NgOptimizedImage` for static images (does NOT work for inline base64).

### State management

- Use **signals** for local/component state; `computed()` for derived state.
- Use `.update()`/`.set()` on signals, never `.mutate()`.
- Keep state transformations pure and predictable.

### Templates

- Use native control flow (`@if`, `@for`, `@switch`) — not `*ngIf`/`*ngFor`/`*ngSwitch`.
- Use `class`/`style` bindings, not `ngClass`/`ngStyle`.
- Use the async pipe to handle observables; import any pipes used in a template.
- Keep templates simple — avoid complex logic.
- Do NOT assume globals like `new Date()` are available in templates.

### Services & DI

- Use the `inject()` function, not constructor injection.
- Singleton services use `providedIn: 'root'`.
- Design services around a single responsibility.

### Host bindings & lazy loading

- Put host bindings in the `host` object of the decorator — do NOT use `@HostBinding`/`@HostListener`.
- Implement lazy loading for feature routes.

### Accessibility

- Must pass all AXE checks and meet WCAG AA minimums (focus management, color contrast, ARIA attributes).

### Change detection / performance

- Prefer zoneless Angular (no zone.js)
- Avoid relying on zone-based change detection
- Use signals and manual triggers instead

### RxJS interop

- Convert observables to signals using toSignal()
- Avoid manual subscriptions in components
- Use async pipe or signals instead

### AI integration

- Structure code to be AI-friendly and analyzable via MCP
- Prefer clear, small, isolated components
- Avoid hidden side effects
- Keep business logic inside services
- Name functions and variables descriptively for AI understanding

### Data access

- Do not call HttpClient directly inside components
- Use dedicated API services
- Keep side effects in services

### Project structure

- Use feature-based folder structure (e.g. /user, /post)
- Each feature contains:
  - components/
  - services/
  - models/
- Avoid cross-feature imports

## Formatting

Prettier: `printWidth: 100`, `singleQuote: true`, Angular parser for `.html`. The base TS config disallows property access from index signatures (`noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature`) — use bracket notation for env vars (e.g. `process.env['PORT']`).
